title: Amauropelta tamandarei
taxonomy: Amauropelta tamandarei is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a family of ferns. It belongs to the genus Amauropelta, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. The classification of Amauropelta tamandarei is further detailed as a member of the order Polypodiales, a large group of ferns with pinnate or divided leaves. At the kingdom level, it falls under Plantae, categorizing it as a plant. The classification of Amauropelta tamandarei is further refined with its species classification, making it a specific and unique member of the plant kingdom.

intro: Amauropelta tamandarei, a species of fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta tamandarei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Amauropelta tamandarei is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
